core_requirements: Strong experience with React, TypeScript, and modern CSS (Flexbox/Grid)
Experience with component-driven development and a design system or component library
Experience with frontend build tooling (Vite, webpack, or similar) and performance optimization
Comfort working directly with product managers to define and iterate on user-facing features
nice_to_have: Experience with state management libraries (Redux, Zustand, or similar)
Familiarity with design tools (Figma) and translating design tokens into code
Experience running lightweight A/B tests or product experiments
summary_paragraph: The role of Frontend Engineer involves developing and enhancing a customer-facing web product within the general software industry. The position requires collaboration with product managers and designers to create polished and accessible user interfaces, with a focus on client-side architecture for a single-page application. The engineer will be responsible for the frontend build pipeline and ensuring the UI remains regression-free through testing. This role emphasizes strong experience with React, TypeScript, and modern CSS, as well as familiarity with accessibility best practices.

matched_skills.evidence: - Build responsive, accessible UI components in React and TypeScript.
- Write component and end-to-end tests to keep the UI regression-free as the product grows.
- Write component and end-to-end tests to keep the UI regression-free as the product grows.
- Experience with component-driven development and a design system or component library.
- Own the frontend build pipeline (Vite/webpack), including bundle size and performance budgets.
